ops.title: How to cut a cc-present release (choreography, the skipped-formula-steps gotcha, brew-cask refresh, version-confirm preference)

ops.body:
Cutting a cc-present release, verified 2026-07-14 shipping v0.9.3 (context-rich option primitives).

**Choreography** (see [[cc-present-push-dance]] for the jj/main dance): main moves under you, so `jj git fetch` + `jj rebase -d main` before advancing. Land the work as **two commits** matching repo pattern — the feature commit, then a separate `chore(plugin): vX.Y.Z` commit that bumps `plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` `version` + edits `CHANGELOG.md` (add `## [X.Y.Z] - DATE` under the empty `## [Unreleased]`, and in the footer swap `[Unreleased]` to `compare/vX.Y.Z...main` and add `[X.Y.Z]: compare/vPREV...vX.Y.Z`). Advance `main` to the chore commit, `jj git push -b main`, then **tag the chore commit** (`git tag vX.Y.Z <hash>`, `git push origin vX.Y.Z`). The release refuses unless the tagged commit is on origin/main, so push main first.

**Release runs** `.github/workflows/release.yml` → reusable `yasyf/homebrew-tap/.github/workflows/release-go.yml@v1` → goreleaser: cross-compiled Go binaries (darwin/linux × amd64/arm64) + go:embed SPA built with bun + GitHub release + **publishes the Homebrew cask to yasyf/homebrew-tap inline**.

**Gotcha — don't be fooled by skipped steps.** In `gh run view`, the job succeeds but `Render the formula`, `Publish the formula to the tap`, and `Import Developer ID certificate` all show skipped (`-`). That is NORMAL: goreleaser's own cask config does the tap publish during the `Release` step, and the macOS signing/notarization secrets are optional (unsigned release when absent). Verify the tap actually updated by reading the cask file version, not those step statuses: `gh api repos/yasyf/homebrew-tap/contents/Casks/cc-present.rb --jq .content | base64 -d | grep version`.

**Local refresh** ("update the local cache"): cc-present installs as a brew **cask** (`Casks/cc-present.rb`, not a formula — `brew list --versions` shows nothing). `brew update && brew upgrade --cask cc-present`, then confirm `cc-present --version`.

**Version convention / preference:** history is minor bumps for feature drops (v0.5.0–v0.9.0), patch for chores/fixes — BUT the user chose a **patch** (v0.9.3) for a feature over the semver-minor recommendation. Confirm the version with the user when cutting a release rather than assuming minor-for-feature.

**Check remote tags immediately before picking the number** (`git ls-remote --tags origin`): concurrent sessions tag releases without changelog entries or plugin.json bumps (2026-07-21: v0.16.0/v0.16.1 daemonkit-repin tags existed while plugin.json still read 0.15.0 and the changelog had no 0.16.x entries — a "chore(plugin): v0.16.0" commit had to be renumbered to v0.17.0 in a follow-up). When that happens, backfill stub entries for the untracked tags (0.14.2 backfill precedent) and check the footer compare-links — concurrent releases skip those too.
